blocks.content: Dust swirled in the great training yard.
It was the dirt kicked up by Rian's legs.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: His heart felt like it would burst.
Air wouldn't come in, and the contents of his stomach were rising up.
What he had eaten for lunch came up through his esophagus.
Rian just let himself vomit.
The only thing he cared about was his two legs.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: A hundred laps! Passed!
He couldn't help but admire how Rian seemed different today.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Damn it! You call this training?!

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: What did you say?
Kite raised his eyes in astonishment.
He was about to give some rare praise, but as they say, a leopard can't change its spots—Rian was complaining without hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I'm still standing, aren't I? Master, don't you have anything tougher?

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Hooh?
Kite was quite surprised.
It had been a long time since Rian had burned with such passion.
It seemed like the first time since he had knelt before Ozent Rai's genius two years ago.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: You... something must have happened?
Rian, with his hands on his knees, raised his head while dripping with sweat.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Nothing happened.
Kite didn't believe him.
In his disciple's eyes, which stared fiercely ahead, there must be the shadow of someone lingering.
'Is it Rai again?'
No matter how he thought about it, the only person who could make Rian like this was the second son of the family.
Had he accomplished something again? But what? Hadn't Rai already mastered his schema?
It didn't matter anyway.
His disciple was Rian, not Rai.
And right now, that disciple was offering his neck, asking to be trained to the limit.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Alright! Let's properly kill you today! I can't take responsibility if you actually die!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: As much as you want!
Rian repeatedly brought down an iron rod twice as heavy as a longsword.
If one couldn't realize the schema through insight, the only way was to force the body to its extreme limit.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Ha! Ha! Ha!
Kite watched with pleasure as Rian repeated his vertical slashes.
But as time passed, his expression hardened.
Finally realizing something, he opened his eyes wide and ran over.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: You crazy fool! Stop! Your muscles will burst!

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: They haven't burst yet!
Kite stopped in his tracks and looked at his disciple.
Tears were streaming down from Rian's eyes as he gritted his teeth and swung the rod.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Damn it! Why won't my muscles burst? Why won't my arms break? I can do more! This isn't the end! I'm not finished yet!
Tears welled up in Kite's eyes too.
He was as frustrated as his disciple.
Why doesn't it work? Even pushing the body to the limits of limits, why can't he open the schema?
Kite wiped his tears with his thick arm.
This wouldn't do.
If the master showed doubt, what would his disciple Rian do?
Kite received Rian's iron rod with his palm as it cut through the air.
Even Rian couldn't insist on continuing when he hit his master's hand and stopped his movement.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Rian, that's enough. Let's stop for today.
At the warm voice, Rian's madness disappeared.
The feeling in his arms had long since vanished.
Without even realizing the club had fallen to the ground, Rian bowed his head deeply and said:

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Yes, thank you.
Kite placed an ice towel on Rian's shoulder.
The fact that his bones weren't damaged showed he was born with a strong physique.
Rian sat on the training ground hill, looking at the distant mountains.
Kite, having finished treating him, sat down beside him.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: What are you thinking about?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: That my arms are killing me.
A smile crossed Kite's lips.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Rian. In the reports I submit to the head of the family, I've never once written that you have talent.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Tch, who asked?

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: But I think you do have talent. Achieving something quickly isn't the only form of talent. Challenging something persistently despite seeming impossible—that too can be called talent.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You don't need to comfort me. That's just a nice way of saying I have no talent, which is why I have to work to death.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Is that really so? Geniuses know they are geniuses. Therefore, they clearly understand what they can do. Have you ever thought about that?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Well... I know well what I can't do.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Yes. That's why you're not a genius.
Rian looked at Kite with an incredulous expression.
Instead of helping a burning house, his master was pouring oil on it, making not just his arms ache but his heart as well.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Stop it. I understand enough already.
As Rian pouted, Kite smiled.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: But, Rian, do you know what geniuses fear most? It's effort. Making an effort ultimately means that you don't have something to begin with. You say you put in effort because you lack talent? Not at all. In this world, the only people who can make an effort are those who make an effort.
Kite grabbed Rian's shoulder and gently pushed strength into it.

blocks.speaker: Kite
blocks.content: Geniuses fear you. Because you have the power to seize what you don't have. Those who make an effort are the natural enemies of geniuses.
The natural enemy of geniuses.
Rian liked this phrase.
Even if it was just to comfort a poor disciple, it didn't matter.
Yes, he simply didn't have it. If he never had it to begin with, he just needed to obtain it with persistence.
Rian clenched his fist.
He intended to pour everything into the remaining month.
Shirone clearly knew what he was capable of.
Even abandoning any attachment to schema and seeking other methods showed his character was the complete opposite of Rian's.
Nevertheless, talent finds the most efficient and shortest path to the destination.
Shirone set aside various techniques and delved only into the basics of swordsmanship.
A month passed quickly.
Eight-directional striking and eight-directional blocking. Having mastered these two skills satisfactorily, Shirone ended his training.
And finally, he asked a question that encapsulated what he had learned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is swordsmanship?
The totality of the movement dynamics that arise with the purpose of killing life. It was also a sophisticated psychology in the sense that it separates life and death.
Shirone imagined a virtual Rian and pointed his sword.
The enemy would likely move from every imaginable angle.
But that was just an illusion.
Only the sword coming to cut him was real.
Shirone responded to his imaginary opponent with imagination.
His sword extended like a phantom, suppressing all eight directions.
After one more exchange, it split into dozens of streams.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not about counting numbers. It's about feeling the whole.
If you focus on the trees, you can't see the forest.
Savoring possibilities as a whole—that was what insight was.
But it wasn't as easy as it sounded.
The more diverse patterns spread out like fractals, the more he found himself fixating on attacks from specific areas.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's okay if I can't achieve it. Let me observe.
Gradually gaining momentum, Shirone completely let go of his thoughts at some point.
All possibilities began to be sucked into the infinite blank space.
Uh? Uhhhhh?
Shirone looked at the world with astonished eyes.
There was no end.
The entire world was being filled with his and Rian's swords.
Unable to handle such immensity, Shirone dropped his sword and staggered.
Haha, hahaha.
A hollow laugh.
Like magic, swordsmanship was also infinite.
He had experienced the infinite world derived from a single sword.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So this is the path swordsmen have chosen.
Wasn't he also walking the infinite path of magic?
An inexplicable sense of kinship made him feel good.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mom, Dad, I'm sorry. I might not be able to come home.
He had no regrets.
Rian would be the same.
Facing the crossroads of life and death, he was thankful to have seen such a wonderful aspect of the world.
— Outside the library, the sound of bells approaching midnight could be heard.
Rian, trying to possess what he couldn't have.
Shirone, who had completed everything he could do.
The clash between the two boys, mirroring each other, was now just an hour away.
